model_comparison: In this comparison, Model 1 (T1) is the clear winner. The user explicitly requested the text to be spoken with 'shouting volume'. T1 successfully modified the vocal dynamics to simulate a genuine shout, with appropriate pitch elevation and projection. T2, on the other hand, delivered the text in a standard speaking voice, failing to increase the volume or projection to a shouting level. Therefore, T1 followed the specific style instruction, while T2 did not.
